intro.title: Who am I?
intro.subTitle: I'm Laszlo Toth, a Software Engineer who builds systems that survive contact with reality.
intro.paragraph: With over a decade of experience, I design and improve software that connects code, data flows, and physical processes - from Laravel-based platforms and distributed integrations to on-site archaeological documentation and early hardware experiments. I'm particularly drawn to the places where systems bend: where data doesn't flow cleanly, where reality introduces friction, and where manual work quietly appears.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subTitle: Where I've Spent the Most Cycles

items.comment: Where messy business logic meets long-lived systems.
A structured layer over chaotic domains.
Understanding systems without the safety net.
Where systems agree to understand each other.
Where truth is stored, approximated, and occasionally negotiated.
Querying systems that don't naturally align.
The glue between intention and interaction.
The structural layer everything else depends on.
Keeping the lights on when the abstractions break.

items.label: Hungarian
items.comment: I can swear in it for 10 minutes straight without repeating a phrase when the build fails at 3 AM.

items.label: English
items.comment: The language I use to explain why my ESP32 is on fire (again).

items.label: German
items.comment: Enough to order beer and understand why bureaucracy is the ultimate legacy system.

education.title: Education
education.subTitle: Self-Directed Learner since 200X
content: Started with an IT-focused high school and an advanced certification, then continued with electrical engineering studies at BME, alongside contributing to Studio Schönherz and the Schönherz Electronics Workshop. Over time, I shifted toward software, where building and iteration felt more immediate.

Since then, most of my learning has come through real systems, production environments, and side experiments across software and hardware. The path hasn't been linear, but it has been consistent: learning by building, and adjusting when reality disagrees.

postScriptum.title: Post Scriptum
postScriptum.subTitle: If you've read this far, thank you. Truly.
content: Putting this page together — and living the experiences it tries to describe — took longer than I expected. Every line is a small attempt to turn messy reality into something a little more understandable. The fact that you stayed with it means a lot.
Whether a particular project, a perspective post, or just a passing detail stuck with you, I'm grateful you took the time.
I'm still the same person who prefers fixing real friction over polishing surfaces, so if anything here feels worth a conversation, I'd be happy to continue it.

contactDetails.title: Get in Touch
contactDetails.subTitle: Let's build something, fix something, or explore something
contactDetails.description: Whether you have an idea, a problem to solve, or just want to connect - feel free to reach out. I'm always open to meaningful conversations and interesting challenges. Tax-registered in Germany and Hungary for cross-border work.
